question_text: Explain why people who do more physical activity require more energy in their daily diet than people who are mostly sedentary.
correct_answer: Physical activity requires the body to transfer more chemical energy from food into other forms of energy (mainly heat and kinetic energy for movement). A sedentary person uses less energy because their body performs fewer energy-demanding processes.
explanation: Energy from food is used to power muscle contractions and movement. The more active a person is, the more energy their muscles need. Additionally, exercise can increase metabolic rate, meaning the body burns more energy even at rest.
